mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates on equity valuations. Higher FEDFUNDS directly compresses forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced present value of future corporate cash flows.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) represent forward-looking demand signal for construction, materials, and consumer durables. A sustained decline in HOUST below 1.8 million units annualized signals weakening housing demand that precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1849.0 thousand units as of April 2006
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST declines > 5% are positive in more than 50% of instances, or correlation between HOUST momentum and SPX 60-day returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal labor market tightness. When UNRATE remains below 5% while PAYEMS growth slows to below 100k/month, this signals wage pressure and profit margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and PAYEMS at 135106k as of May 2006
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint condition) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic output capacity utilization. A 3-month decline in INDPRO signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking equity markets price in reduced earnings growth.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following INDPRO 3-month declines > 0.5% are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and real GDP (GDPC1) jointly capture aggregate demand strength. When RSAFS growth decelerates below 3% annualized while GDPC1 growth remains above 2%, this signals demand weakness concentrated in consumer spending that will compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth < 3% annualized AND GDPC1 > 2% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 364198.0 million dollars and GDPC1 at 11394.7 billion as of May/March 2006
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power beyond univariate RSAFS models (R² improvement < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% combined with elevated FEDFUNDS signals persistent monetary tightening that will continue to compress equity valuations. This dual signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets expect further rate maintenance or increases.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 201.9 and FEDFUNDS at 4.94% as of May 2006
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the joint inflation-rate signal shows no better predictive power than FEDFUNDS alone (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) is an always-on trend signal. Rising UNRATE signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and equity multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The relationship is monotonic: each 0.1pp increase in UNRATE corresponds to lower forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between UNRATE and 20-day SPX forward returns is not significantly negative (Spearman rho > -0.15, p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer-driven demand. When both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously by more than 3% month-over-month, this signals synchronized demand collapse across housing and consumption that produces sharply negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity markets reprice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 3% MoM AND RSAFS declines > 3% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1849.0 and RSAFS at 364198.0 as of April/May 2006
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.015).
